label: Tech Lead, Sustainability Tech
text:
Tech Lead — Sustainability Tech (Remote, Europe)

We are an early-stage climate-tech company building tooling for supply chain transparency in agricultural commodities. We work with global FMCG brands and producer cooperatives to verify ethical sourcing and trace product provenance from farm to shelf.

We're hiring our first technical lead to take ownership of our data platform and customer-facing analytics. You'll set technical direction, mentor a small engineering team, and partner closely with our domain experts and customer-success leads.

Responsibilities:
• Lead architecture for our data ingestion and analytics stack — Python, Postgres, modern orchestration (Dagster or similar)
• Own the customer-facing analytics dashboard end-to-end — React/TypeScript
• Establish data quality and observability practices across messy, real-world supply chain data
• Mentor a team of 3–6 engineers; set hiring bar
• Work directly with non-technical stakeholders to translate domain requirements into systems
• Set us up for AI/ML feature work over the next 12 months

Requirements:
• 6+ years building production software, full-stack preferred
• Deep React + TypeScript experience
• Strong Python; bonus for Dagster, Airflow, dbt, or similar
• Comfortable making architectural decisions in ambiguity
• Experience with multi-tenant SaaS or B2B platforms
• Track record mentoring or leading engineers
• Background in supply chain, sustainability, agritech, or fintech a plus
• Prior experience with graph databases or anomaly-detection systems welcome
• Familiarity with on-chain provenance / Ethereum a nice-to-have
• EU-based or willing to overlap with European hours

What we offer competitive equity, mission-led work, async culture, four-day discovery sprints quarterly.

chips.reasoning: 7+ years across OpenSC and Redington, both production systems with paying customers.
Sole frontend on the OpenSC analytics dashboard; owned architectural decisions on the Redington multi-tenant TS dashboard.
Re-architected OpenSC supply-chain onboarding in Dagster with a YAML-configured, data-contract-driven layer.
Built EPCIS standardisation for first-three-miles farm data; surfaced infiltration / exfiltration / untraced origins for Nespresso and Austral Fisheries.
Built monitoring for data quality, ingestion accuracy, and frequency across all supply chain feeds.
Owned architecture on a multi-tenant research platform used by thousands of consultants across 7+ tenants — cross-tenant leakage would have been existential.
Surfaced supply chain anomalies through graph-based analysis powered by Kuzu — direct match.

chips.text: Mentor / lead a small engineering team
chips.reasoning: Led a 5-person frontend team at Redington and ran the LifeCycle pro-bono engagement end-to-end. Title was team-lead rather than tech-lead, so generosity matters here.

Sole frontend engineer at OpenSC + co-led architectural decisions at Redington both involved direction-setting under ambiguity, but neither role formally carried 'set technical direction for the company'.
OpenSC stack listed Ethereum, but the CV doesn't claim primary ownership of the on-chain layer. Strict reading would call this a miss; generous would credit production exposure.
chips.gapFraming: Worked alongside an Ethereum-touching stack at OpenSC but didn't own the on-chain layer myself. Happy to talk about exposure vs ownership if that distinction matters here.

chips.text: Set hiring bar / formal hiring authority
chips.reasoning: Team-lead at Redington implies interview involvement, but the CV doesn't claim ownership of a hiring bar. Honest gap; generous reading credits adjacent experience.
chips.gapFraming: I led a 5-engineer team but the CV doesn't speak to setting a hiring bar — happy to talk about the interviews I ran and how I'd approach this if it's load-bearing for the role.

chips.reasoning: Nespresso/Austral integration work and the LifeCycle charity engagement were stakeholder-heavy, but the CV foregrounds the engineering rather than the relationship-management.

label: Senior Full-Stack at AI startup
text:
Senior Full-Stack Engineer — Series A AI Startup

We're building agent-native developer tooling. Looking for a senior full-stack engineer who's been hands-on with LLMs in production and has strong opinions about agent harness design, prompt security, and what good orchestration looks like.

You'll work directly with the founders. Small team, fast cycles, async, equity-heavy.

Requirements:
• 5+ years full-stack (React/TS + Python or Node)
• Hands-on production LLM work — agents, tool-use, RAG, evals
• Familiar with prompt injection, jailbreaks, OWASP AI Top 10
• Comfortable with ambiguity; can hold the whole stack in your head
• Bonus: open-source agent harnesses, custom Claude/Cursor setups, MCP servers
• Bonus: developer-tool background

chips.reasoning: 7+ years across OpenSC + Redington.
Primary stack across both roles.
Currently training in OWASP AI Top 10 and prompt injection; not yet shipped to production.
Three side projects in flight using LLMs; no production agent system shipped at scale.
chips.gapFraming: Side-project LLM work in flight; nothing production-grade at scale yet. The work I'd describe as production was the Claude Code setup and tooling — happy to walk through it.

chips.reasoning: Personal Claude CLI tooling with agents, skills, and telemetry, listed in Skills. Not a shipped product, so a Stretch rather than a Hit.
chips.gapFraming: Tooling rather than a product — happy to walk through it.

chips.reasoning: No production evals on the CV.
chips.gapFraming: Reading + experimenting with eval frameworks; nothing production-grade yet. Honest gap.

chips.reasoning: Stated current focus area; the personal Claude Code tooling (agents, skills, telemetry) is the closest thing to harness work, but it isn't a shipped product.
Not on the CV.
chips.gapFraming: Have read the spec, haven't built one. Could spike a working server in a day or two if it'd help the conversation.

chips.text: Hold whole stack in your head
chips.reasoning: Sole frontend on OpenSC analytics; full E2E ownership.
No dev-tool company on the CV.
chips.gapFraming: Personal Claude Code setup is dev-tooling-shaped, but I haven't been paid to build dev tools.

label: Frontend Lead, Fintech
text:
Frontend Lead — Fintech (London / hybrid)

Established UK fintech (Series C) hiring a frontend lead to own the architecture of our customer-facing platform and lead a team of 6.

Requirements:
• 6+ years frontend, deep React + TypeScript
• Multi-tenant or sensitive-data experience
• Has led a frontend team
• Strong testing culture (Cypress / Playwright)
• Financial services background a strong plus
• London or willing to relocate / hybrid 2 days/week

chips.reasoning: 7+ years frontend leadership across OpenSC + Redington.
Primary stack on every role.
Owned architecture on Redington's multi-tenant platform with cross-tenant-existential risk.
Led 5-engineer frontend team at Redington — direct match.
Drove Cypress E2E adoption at Redington.
Three years at Redington, a UK investment consultancy — direct match.
Owned + co-led architectural decisions on the Redington frontend.
Led 5; one short of stated 6. Stretch under strict reading.
Currently Valencia-based, remote.
chips.gapFraming: Valencia-based and remote-first. Open to fly-in cadence — happy to discuss what would actually work.

chips.reasoning: Cypress on the CV, not Playwright.
chips.gapFraming: Cypress experience reads across; haven't shipped Playwright but would be a quick ramp.
